description: Real-world coding tasks on the Kontexta monorepo, comparing token consumption with and without MCP-assisted context retrieval.
methodology: Baseline ('without Kontexta') reflects the files a developer would realistically open in their IDE for each task — not the entire codebase. 'With Kontexta' reflects only the files the AI actually retrieved via MCP tool calls. Token counts use the cl100k_base tokenizer (GPT-4 / Claude-equivalent).

scenarios.name: Implement master-password authentication
scenarios.description: Add a secure login system with session cookies, IP bypass rules, and protected API routes.

without_kontexta.rationale: Developer opens the main app entry, all existing API routes, DB layer, and related hooks.

with_kontexta.rationale: Kontexta search('auth login session') + read specific DB and layout files only.

scenarios.name: Add a new REST API endpoint
scenarios.description: Create a new API route following the existing project conventions (auth, DB, validation).

without_kontexta.rationale: Developer opens several existing route examples to understand patterns before writing.

with_kontexta.rationale: Kontexta search('api route handler') returns 1–2 example routes + db-init.

scenarios.name: Debug WebSocket reconnection logic
scenarios.description: Investigate why WebSocket disconnects and fix the reconnection / auth handling.

without_kontexta.rationale: Developer opens all hooks, the WS server, and the top-level component to trace the data flow.

with_kontexta.rationale: Kontexta search('websocket reconnect') returns only the WS server and hook.

scenarios.name: Understand the database schema & migrations
scenarios.description: A new contributor wants to understand how the SQLite schema evolves across migrations.

without_kontexta.rationale: New developer opens the entire DB module and all related metadata modules.

with_kontexta.rationale: Kontexta search('migration schema database') returns db/index and settings only.
